description: Logically-backed socio-economic indicator model for crime correlation. Each indicator has a precise definition, an exact derivation from Census 2011 columns (no vague aggregates), a unit and plausible range, and an explicit, theory-grounded hypothesis about how it relates to specific crime GROUPS in crime_taxonomy.json. Indicators join to crime data on geo_unit_id at DISTRICT grain.

join.crime_link: Each indicator is correlated against crime-group rates per 100k (groups from crime_taxonomy.json), computed for the same district and the latest crime year.
join.note: Socio-economic values are Census 2011 vintage; crime rates use the latest available crime year. Both are tied to the same canonical district geo_unit_id.

ethical_constraints.ecological_fallacy: All relationships are DISTRICT-level (ecological). A district-level correlation does NOT license any inference about individuals. Never use these to profile a person, group, or community.
ethical_constraints.recorded_vs_true_crime: Crime figures are RECORDED crime and reflect reporting and policing intensity, not necessarily underlying crime. Higher recorded crime can mean better reporting.
ethical_constraints.non_causality: Correlation is not causation. These indicators are explanatory context for analysts, never a justification for enforcement targeting.
ethical_constraints.protected_attributes: scst_share and sex_ratio are included only to explain VICTIMIZATION/REPORTING patterns (e.g. atrocities against SC/ST, crimes against women). They must NEVER be read as group propensity to offend. Treat with the highest caution and human review.
ethical_constraints.small_sample: n = 30 districts. Correlations are indicative, not definitive; always report the p-value and confidence.

correlation_method.target: crime-group rate per 100,000 population (and overall crime rate)
correlation_method.hypothesis_check: Each computed correlation is compared to the indicator's hypothesized sign for that crime group; 'confirmed' if same sign and significant, 'contradicted' if opposite sign and significant, else 'inconclusive'.

indicators.name: Population Density
indicators.definition: Resident population per square kilometre of district land area.
indicators.derivation: population (Census 2011) ÷ area_km2 (computed geodesically from the district boundary polygon).

hypotheses.theory: Routine Activity Theory — denser areas concentrate suitable targets and motivated offenders; more opportunity.
Social Disorganization — density correlates with anonymity and weaker informal social control.
Urban opportunity structure and target density.
Urban economic activity expands fraud/cheating surface.

indicators.name: Literacy Rate
indicators.definition: Literate persons as a share of total population.

hypotheses.theory: Social Control / human capital — education raises opportunity cost of crime and strengthens norms.

indicators.name: Graduate Education Rate
indicators.definition: Graduates as a share of total population.

hypotheses.theory: Human capital — higher education associated with lower violent offending.

indicators.name: Low-Income Household Share (poverty proxy)
indicators.definition: Share of households in the lowest income-equivalent bracket (< Rs 45,000/year, PPP-style).

hypotheses.theory: Strain Theory / economic deprivation — material hardship raises acquisitive crime.

indicators.name: Marginal Worker Share (underemployment proxy)
indicators.definition: Marginal (works < 6 months/yr) workers as a share of all workers.

hypotheses.theory: Economic precarity and seasonal income gaps (Strain).

indicators.name: Non-Worker Share (joblessness/dependency proxy)
indicators.definition: Non-workers as a share of total population.
indicators.derivation: Census 2011. NOTE: includes students, homemakers, elderly — a dependency proxy, NOT a clean unemployment rate.

indicators.name: Work Participation Rate
indicators.definition: Workers as a share of total population.

indicators.name: SC/ST Population Share
indicators.definition: Scheduled Caste + Scheduled Tribe persons as a share of population.

hypotheses.theory: Higher SC/ST population is associated with more REPORTED atrocities under the SC/ST (PoA) Act — a victimization/reporting relationship, NOT offending.
Some literature links skewed (low) sex ratios to higher crimes against women; direction uncertain and confounded by reporting. Interpret with extreme caution.

indicators.name: Internet Penetration (development proxy)
indicators.definition: Households with internet as a share of all households.

hypotheses.theory: Connectivity expands the economic/cyber-enabled fraud surface.
